sanskrit:
अकीर्तिं चापि भूतानि कथयिष्यन्ति तेऽव्ययाम्।
संभावितस्य चाकीर्तिर्मरणादतिरिच्यते॥
transliteration:
akīrtiṃ cāpi bhūtāni kathayiṣyanti te'vyayām |
saṃbhāvitasya cākīrtir maraṇād atiricyate ||
literal_meaning: Ever-lasting infamy all beings will proclaim of you; for an honored man such dishonor outweighs death.
contextual_meaning: Krṣṇa confronts Arjuna’s avoidance by reframing it as a social-identity catastrophe: the psyche will suffer more from perpetual shame than from physical death.
psychological_state: Pre-emptive shame spiral: the mind anticipates irreparable damage to self-esteem and belonging, making psychic death feel worse than physical death.
trigger_scenario: A respected professional contemplates whistle-blowing; the dread of lifelong reputation loss feels more terrifying than the risk of physical retaliation.
anchor_text: Choose action over shame; disgrace haunts longer than death.
